title: practical english usage - Part 279
content:
6  For the contraction let's, ►    225.

lames ain't been here for days.

I ain't got no more cigarettes.

'It's raining.' 'No it ain't.' I ain't going to tell him. Don't talk to me like that - you ain't my boss.

am not, are not, is not, have not and has not.

5 In non-standard English, ain't (pronounced /eɪnt/) is used as a contraction of English; needn't is rare in American English.

4  Daren't, mightn't, oughtn't, shan't and usedn't are rare, especially in American American English (/kænt/).
